quizSrc.term: Company XYZ provides consulting services to Company ABC in November 2022 for a fee of $100,000. Company ABC pays $50,000 in cash in November 2022 and the remaining $50,000 in January 2023. Company XYZ incurred $10,000 in expenses related to the consulting services in November 2022, which will be paid in December 2022. Additionally, on November 1, 2022, Company XYZ pre-paid $12,000 for rent for the next twelve months. What should Company XYZ recognize as net income/ net operating cash flow for November 2022? A. Net Income: $100,000, Net Operating Cash Flow: $50,000. B. Net Income: $89,000; Net Operating Cash Flow: $50,000. C. Net Income: $88,000; Net Operating Cash Flow: $48,000. D. Net Income: $89,000; Net Operating Cash Flow: $38,000.
quizSrc.definition: D. Net Income: $89,000; Net Operating Cash Flow: $38,000.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is NOT an objective of the Conceptual Framework, as defined by US GAAP? A. To enhance consistency and comparability in financial reporting B. To provide a framework for standard-setting C. To assist preparers of financial statements in applying US GAAP D. To establish specific rules for recognizing and measuring transactions and events
quizSrc.definition: D. To establish specific rules for recognizing and measuring transactions and events

quizSrc.term: Which factors would raise doubts about an entity's ability to continue as a going concern under US GAAP? A. A history of profitable operations and strong cash flows. B. A significant decrease in demand for the entity's products or services. C. A stable industry with no significant competitive pressures. D. A strong balance sheet with high levels of liquidity and low levels of debt.
quizSrc.definition: B. A significant decrease in demand for the entity's products or services.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following statements regarding the use of IFRS in the United States is true? A. All US companies must use IFRS for financial reporting purposes. B. The US has fully adopted IFRS and no longer requires the use of US GAAP. C. Only foreign subsidiaries of US companies are allowed to use IFRS. D. US companies can voluntarily choose to use IFRS for financial reporting purposes.
quizSrc.definition: D. US companies can voluntarily choose to use IFRS for financial reporting purposes.

quizSrc.term: Which organization is primarily responsible for establishing and improving US Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP)? A. The International Accounting Standards Board (IASB) B. The American Institute of Certified Public Accountants (AICPA) C. The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) D. The Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB)
quizSrc.definition: D. The Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB)

quizSrc.term: ABC Corporation has been using the FIFO inventory valuation method for several years. They have decided to switch to the LIFO method in the current year. Which accounting principle requires that they disclose this change in their financial statements? A. Neutrality B. Consistency C. The matching principle D. Materiality
quizSrc.definition: B. Consistency

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is an example of the historical cost principle in accounting? A. Recording the fair value of a stock investment on the balance sheet B. Recording the value of inventory at its current market price C. Recording a machine on the balance sheet at its original purchase price D. Recording the estimated market value of a company's trademarks on the balance sheet
quizSrc.definition: C. Recording a machine on the balance sheet at its original purchase price

quizSrc.term: As per Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), financial statements are the primary means of communicating financial information to stakeholders. Which of the following is not classified as a financial statement? A. Income statement B. Statement of Comprehensive Income C. Notes D. Balance sheet
quizSrc.definition: C. Notes

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is an example of accrual accounting? A. A company records revenue when it delivers products or services to customers, regardless of when it receives payment. B. A company records revenue only when it receives cash payments from customers. C. A company records expenses when it receives bills from suppliers. D. A company records expenses when it pays cash for goods and services.
quizSrc.definition: A. A company records revenue when it delivers products or services to customers, regardless of when it receives payment.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following scenarios demonstrates the use of accrual accounting? A. A business records a $10,000 sale on credit on December 31st, but the payment is not received until January 15th of the following year. B. A business records a $2,000 expense for rent on December 31st, but the payment is not due until February 1st of the following year. C. A business records a $3,000 cash sale on December 31st and deposits the cash on the same day. D. A business purchases $5,000 worth of inventory on credit on December 31st, but pays for it in cash on January 5th of the following year.
quizSrc.definition: A. A business records a $10,000 sale on credit on December 31st, but the payment is not received until January 15th of the following year.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is an example of an accounting process? A. Posting a job opening on a company website B. Calculating payroll taxes for employees C. Researching new marketing strategies for a business D. Ordering inventory for a retail store
quizSrc.definition: B. Calculating payroll taxes for employees

quizSrc.term: Which of the following best describes a T-account? A. A record of all financial transactions in a business B. A summary of all accounts in the general ledger C. A graphical representation of debits and credits for a specific account D. A statement of financial position of a company at a specific point in time
quizSrc.definition: C. A graphical representation of debits and credits for a specific account

quizSrc.term: What is the purpose of an adjusting entry in the accounting process? A. To correct errors in the general ledger B. To record transactions that have not yet been posted C. To close out temporary accounts at the end of the accounting period D. To update accounts to their proper amounts for financial reporting
quizSrc.definition: D. To update accounts to their proper amounts for financial reporting

quizSrc.term: Company X's retained earnings balance at the end of 2024 was $60,000. During that year, the company paid out $10,000 in dividends and had a net income of $25,000. What was the company's retained earnings balance at the beginning of 2024? A. $45,000 B. $55,000 C. $75,000 D. $95,000

If a company borrows money from a bank, how does this transaction affect the company's liabilities and assets? A. Liabilities decrease and assets increase B. Liabilities increase and assets decrease C. Liabilities and assets both decrease D. Liabilities and assets both increase
quizSrc.definition: D. Liabilities and assets both increase

quizSrc.term: Which of the following best describes the revenue recognition concept in accounting? A. Recognizing revenue when it is earned, regardless of when payment is received B. Recognizing revenue when the product is shipped to the customer C. Recognizing revenue when the customer places an order D. Recognizing revenue when cash is received
quizSrc.definition: A. Recognizing revenue when it is earned, regardless of when payment is received

quizSrc.term: Which of the following statements best describes a non-adjusted trial balance? A. It includes all transactions that occurred during the accounting period B. It is the final step in the accounting cycle C. It includes only the ending balances of all accounts D. It is prepared before any adjusting entries are made
quizSrc.definition: D. It is prepared before any adjusting entries are made

quizSrc.term: Which of the following statements is true about closing entries in accounting? A. Closing entries are made at the beginning of the accounting period to record opening balances. B. Closing entries are used to close permanent accounts only. C. Closing entries transfer net income or loss to the retained earnings account. D. Closing entries are optional and unnecessary for preparing financial statements.
quizSrc.definition: C. Closing entries transfer net income or loss to the retained earnings account.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is an example of an item that may be included in the statement of comprehensive income? A. Dividends paid to shareholders B. Interest income earned on investments C. Purchase of a new office building D. Unrealized gains in available for sale investments
quizSrc.definition: D. Unrealized gains in available for sale investments

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is an example of a permanent asset account in the accounting process? A. Interest income B. Prepaid rent C. Wages payable D. Supplies expense
quizSrc.definition: B. Prepaid rent

quizSrc.term: At the end of the 2022 fiscal year, Company X has current assets of $800,000, including inventory of $120,000 and prepaid rent of $50,000. Its total assets are $1,500,000, current liabilities are $200,000, and total liabilities are $400,000. What is Company X's acid test? A. 1.2 B. 2.8 C. 3.15 D. 4.5

At the end of the 2022 fiscal year, Company X has current assets of $800,000, including inventory of $120,000 and prepaid rent of $50,000. Its total assets are $1,500,000, current liabilities are $200,000, and total liabilities are $400,000. What is Company X's debt to equity ratio? A. 0.27 B. 1.36 C. 2.75 D. 0.36

Which of the following best describes the times interest earned (TIE) ratio? A. It measures a company's ability to pay its short-term debts as they come due. B. It measures a company's ability to generate profits relative to its invested capital. C. It measures a company's ability to meet its interest obligations. D. It measures a company's ability to pay off all of its debts in the next fiscal year.
quizSrc.definition: C. It measures a company's ability to meet its interest obligations.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following policy decisions would not be included in the Summary of Significant Accounting Policies? A. Revenue recognition for long-term construction contracts B. Depreciation method C. Issuance of debt or equity D. Costing method for inventory
quizSrc.definition: C. Issuance of debt or equity

quizSrc.term: Given the importance of liquidity ratios in measuring a company's ability to meet its short-term obligations, which of the following transactions would contribute to an increase in a company's liquidity ratios? A. Pay dividends to shareholders B. Borrow cash by signing a two-year note C. Purchase inventories with cash D. Pay suppliers with cash
quizSrc.definition: B. Borrow cash by signing a two-year note

quizSrc.term: Which of the following statements accurately categorizes accumulated depreciation in the Balance Sheet, a financial statement that provides information about a company's financial position at a specific point in time? A. A liability category that includes long-term debt obligations, such as loans or bonds. B. A contra-asset category that offsets the value of an asset, such as property or equipment, and reflects the total depreciation expense incurred over the asset's useful life. C. A contra liability category that offsets the value of a liability, such as accounts payable or notes payable. D. An asset category that reflects the value of an asset, such as property or equipment, after accounting for depreciation.
quizSrc.definition: B. A contra-asset category that offsets the value of an asset, such as property or equipment, and reflects the total depreciation expense incurred over the asset's useful life.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following factors hinders the efficacy of the Balance Sheet for internal and external decision makers? A. The Balance Sheet utilizes assumptions to determine Balance Sheet values. B. The Balance Sheet reports amounts at a specific point in time instead of covering an entire period. C. The Balance Sheet doesn't provide information regarding the company's revenues and expenses. D. The Balance Sheet is not reviewed as frequently by an independent entity as the other financial statements.
quizSrc.definition: A. The Balance Sheet utilizes assumptions to determine Balance Sheet values.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following statements is true regarding the differences between Form 10-K, Form 10-Q, and Form 8-K? A. Form 10-K is filed annually and includes audited financial statements, while Form 10-Q is filed quarterly and includes unaudited financial statements. B. Form 10-Q is filed annually and includes audited financial statements, while Form 10-K is filed quarterly and includes unaudited financial statements. C. Form 10-K and Form 10-Q are both filed quarterly and include audited financial statements, while Form 8-K is filed annually and includes unaudited financial statements. D. Form 8-K is filed annually and includes audited financial statements, while Form 10-K is filed quarterly and includes unaudited financial statements.
quizSrc.definition: A. Form 10-K is filed annually and includes audited financial statements, while Form 10-Q is filed quarterly and includes unaudited financial statements.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following statements is true regarding the difference between unqualified and qualified audit reports? A. An unqualified audit report indicates that there were no significant deficiencies or material weaknesses found in the audit, while a qualified audit report indicates that there were some deficiencies but they were not material. B. An unqualified audit report indicates that the financial statements are free of material misstatements, while a qualified audit report indicates that there were material misstatements found in the financial statements. C. An unqualified audit report indicates that the financial statements are presented fairly in accordance with GAAP, while a qualified audit report indicates that the financial statements were not prepared in accordance with GAAP. D. An unqualified audit report indicates that the auditor is unable to express an opinion on the financial statements, while a qualified audit report indicates that the auditor has expressed an adverse opinion on the financial statements.
quizSrc.definition: A. An unqualified audit report indicates that there were no significant deficiencies or material weaknesses found in the audit, while a qualified audit report indicates that there were some deficiencies but they were not material.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is an example of a segment as defined by the segment reporting standards? A. Product line B. Headquarters location C. Type of employee benefit plan D. Office furniture
quizSrc.definition: A. Product line

quizSrc.term: What is the difference between a single-step income statement and a multiple-step income statement? A. A single-step income statement is used for small businesses, while a multiple-step income statement is used for large corporations. B. A single-step income statement is more detailed than a multiple-step income statement. C. A single-step income statement is easier to understand than a multiple-step income statement. D. A single-step income statement combines all revenues and expenses into one category, while a multiple-step income statement separates them into several categories.
quizSrc.definition: D. A single-step income statement combines all revenues and expenses into one category, while a multiple-step income statement separates them into several categories.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is an example of other income (expense) on the income statement? A. Cost of goods sold B. Revenue from the sale of goods or services C. Wages and salaries expense D. Interest Revenue
quizSrc.definition: D. Interest Revenue

quizSrc.term: What is the difference between continued operations and discontinued operations on an income statement? A. Continued operations are business activities that have a significant impact on the financial statements, while discontinued operations are business activities that have little or no impact on the financial statements. B. Continued operations are ongoing business activities that are expected to continue in the future, while discontinued operations are business activities that have been sold or terminated. C. Continued operations are business activities that generate a profit, while discontinued operations are business activities that generate a loss. D. Continued operations are non-recurring business activities that occur regularly, while discontinued operations are recurring business activities that are expected to continue in the future.
quizSrc.definition: B. Continued operations are ongoing business activities that are expected to continue in the future, while discontinued operations are business activities that have been sold or terminated.

quizSrc.term: What is the formula for calculating earnings per share (EPS)? A. Total liabilities divided by total equity B. Net income divided by total revenue C. Net income minus preferred dividends divided by average common shares outstanding D. Net income divided by total assets
quizSrc.definition: C. Net income minus preferred dividends divided by average common shares outstanding

quizSrc.term: A company has a net income of $500,000 and non-convertible preferred stock dividends of $50,000. The company has 1,000,000 shares of common stock outstanding for the entire year and also has stock options outstanding that are convertible into 100,000 shares of common stock. What is the diluted earnings per share (EPS) for the company? A. $0.50 B. $0.55 C. $0.45 D. $0.41

Which of the following is not a commonly used non-GAAP earnings metric? A. Gross Profit Margin B. Adjusted Earnings C. EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization) D. Non-Recurring Expenses
quizSrc.definition: A. Gross Profit Margin

quizSrc.term: Using the indirect method, what is the cash flow from operating activities based on the following information? Net income = $15,000 Loss on sale of equipment = $3,500 Increase in accounts receivable = $1,200 Decrease in inventory = $400 Increase in accounts payable = $700 A. $16,000 B. $14,200 C. $13,600 D. $18,400

What is the difference between the direct and indirect method of preparing the cash flow statement? A. The direct method reports cash inflows and outflows from investing activities, while the indirect method reports changes in cash and cash equivalents. B. The direct method reports cash inflows and outflows from financing activities, while the indirect method reports changes in income and expenses. C. The direct method reports actual cash inflows and outflows from operating activities, while the indirect method adjusts net income for non-cash items. D. The direct method reports cash inflows and outflows from operating activities, while the indirect method reports changes in balance sheet accounts.
quizSrc.definition: C. The direct method reports actual cash inflows and outflows from operating activities, while the indirect method adjusts net income for non-cash items.

quizSrc.term: A company has accounts receivable of $50,000 and credit sales of $200,000 for the year. What is the average collection period for accounts receivable? (round to the nearest number) A. 90 days B. 120 days C. 30 days D. 60 days
quizSrc.definition: A. 90 days

quizSrc.term: A company has a net profit margin of 8%, an asset turnover of 1.5, and an equity multiplier of 2. What is the return on equity (ROE)? A. 24% B. 36% C. 12% D. 16%

Emily has $5,000 that she wants to invest in a savings account that earns 3% interest compounded annually for a period of four years. What is the expected future value of her investment at the end of the four-year term? A. $5,615 B. $5,769 C. $5,627 D. $6,337

Which of the following best describes the concept of time value of money? A. Money is worth more today than the same amount in the future due to inflation. B. Money is worth less today than the same amount in the future due to inflation. C. Money is worth more in the future than the same amount today due to the potential to earn interest. D. Money has the same value today and in the future, regardless of the timing of the cash flow.
quizSrc.definition: C. Money is worth more in the future than the same amount today due to the potential to earn interest.

quizSrc.term: David wants to invest $10,000 in a savings account that earns 4% simple interest per year for two years. What is the expected future value of his investment at the end of the two-year term? A. $10,400 B. $10,800 C. $11,200 D. $11,600

What is the key difference between an ordinary annuity, an annuity due, and a deferred annuity? A. The type of investment vehicle B. The timing of the payments C. The amount of the payments D. The frequency of the payments
quizSrc.definition: B. The timing of the payments

quizSrc.term: The Smith Corporation wants to accumulate $3,500,000 for a new project. The funds are needed on January 1, 2027. Smith intends to make six equal annual deposits in a fund that will earn interest at 8% compounded annually. The first deposit is to be made one year from today, January 1, 2022. What is the amount of the required annual deposit? A. $477,104 B. $678,405 C. $441,964 D. $580,122

Carla plans to make monthly deposits of $150 for four years into a savings account. The first deposit will be made immediately. The savings account pays interest at an annual rate of 6%, compounded monthly. How much will Carla have accumulated in the savings account at the end of the four-year period? Round to the nearest dollar. A. $7,305 B. $8,115 C. $8,155 D. $9,011

On January 1, 2022, the Johnson Company purchased merchandise from a supplier. Payment was a non-interest bearing note requiring four annual payments of $30,000 on each December 31 beginning on December 31, 2022, and a lump-sum payment of $120,000 on December 31, 2025. A 12% interest rate properly reflects the time value of money in this situation. How much should you capitalize the merchandise purchase in the assets? A. $150,000 B. $155,622 C. $165,000 D. $167,383

The Kiddo Toy Company needs to acquire the use of a new piece of equipment to enhance its manufacturing process. The machine in question is manufactured by the Bouncy Corp. The machine has an estimated useful life of 15 years and can be sold for $20,000 at the end of its useful life. Bouncy Corp has provided Kiddo Toy Company with two options: Buy the Machine. The equipment can be purchased outright for $300,000. The machine's annual maintenance and insurance cost is expected to be around $10,000 per year, which Kiddo Toy Company would pay. Lease the Machine. The machine can be leased for 15 years with an annual lease payment of $35,000. The first payment is due immediately. Bouncy Corp will cover all maintenance and insurance costs. At the end of the 15-year lease period, the machine would be returned to Bouncy Corp. If the time value of money is estimated to be 10%, which option should Kiddo Toy Company choose? A. It doesn't matter as the costs are the same. B. Lease the machine, as it is cheaper in the long run. C. Buy the machine, as it is cheaper in the long run. D. Insufficient information to determine the best option.
quizSrc.definition: B. Lease the machine, as it is cheaper in the long run.

quizSrc.term: Widget Co. needs to acquire the use of a piece of equipment to manufacture its products. The equipment needed is manufactured by Gear Inc. The equipment can be used for 7 years and then sold for $20,000 at the end of its useful life. Gear has presented Widget with the following options: Buy the equipment. The equipment could be purchased for $200,000 in cash. All maintenance costs, which approximate $10,000 per year, would be paid by Widget. Lease the equipment. The equipment could be leased for a 7-year period for an annual lease payment of $40,000 with the first payment due immediately. All maintenance costs will be paid for by the Gear Inc. and the equipment will revert back to Gear at the end of the 7-year period. Assuming that a 10% interest rate properly reflects the time value of money in this situation and that all maintenance costs are paid at the end of each year, determine which option Widget should choose for its present value. Ignore taxes. A. $201.456.37 B. $214,210.43 C. $238,421.03 D. $270,000.00

As a high-profile executive of a Fortune 500 company, Elizabeth has been asked to accept an early retirement package. The company has offered her three enticing compensation packages to persuade her to retire. The packages are as follows: A lump sum of $500,000 to be paid immediately. An annuity of $50,000 per year for 25 years, with the first payment made immediately. An annuity of $100,000 per year for 10 years, with the first payment made on the date Elizabeth turns 65 years old (in 10 years). Assuming that Elizabeth can invest funds at a 8% rate, which alternative should she choose? A. Option 1: $500,000 cash payment B. Option 2: Annuity of $50,000 per year for 25 years, with the first payment made immediately C. Option 3: An annuity of $100,000 per year for 10 years, with the first payment made on the date Elizabeth turns 65 years old (in 10 years).
quizSrc.definition: B. Option 2: Annuity of $50,000 per year for 25 years, with the first payment made immediately

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is a correct statement regarding revenue recognition under the accrual basis of accounting? A. Revenue is recognized when cash is collected from customers. B. Revenue is recognized when the product or service is delivered to customers. C. Revenue is recognized when the company receives a purchase order from a customer. D. Revenue is recognized when the customer places an order for the product or service.
quizSrc.definition: B. Revenue is recognized when the product or service is delivered to customers.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following statements regarding performance obligations under US GAAP is true? A. A performance obligation can only be satisfied when a customer has paid in full B. A performance obligation must be satisfied over time C. A performance obligation must be satisfied at a specific point in time D. A performance obligation is a promise to transfer a distinct good or service to a customer
quizSrc.definition: D. A performance obligation is a promise to transfer a distinct good or service to a customer

quizSrc.term: On December 1, 2022, ABC Corp signed a contract with a customer for $12,000 to provide services over the next six months. On December 31, 2022, ABC completed 20% of the services. Which of the following journal entries should ABC make on December 31, 2022, in accordance with US GAAP revenue recognition standards? A. Debit Cash for $2,400, credit Revenue for $2,400 B. Debit Accounts Receivable for $2,400, credit Revenue for $2,400 C. Debit Unearned Revenue for $9,600, credit Revenue for $9,600 D. Debit Unearned Revenue for $2,400, credit Revenue for $2,400
quizSrc.definition: D. Debit Unearned Revenue for $2,400, credit Revenue for $2,400

quizSrc.term: In the realm of commerce and contractual obligations, Dunne Co. has crafted an agreement comprising two remarkable performance obligations. Behold Product A, with a majestic stand-alone selling price of $150, and Product B, graced with a stand-alone selling price of $300. Now, witness the grandeur of their combined offering, shimmering at a total price of $360. Enveloped in this transaction lies the question: What portion of this splendid price shall be artfully allocated to the performance obligation for delivering Product B? A. $90 B. $240 C. $150 D. $120

The prestigious Cora Co. has inked a wondrous agreement in contractual marvels and corporate dealings, graced with two distinct performance obligations. Its standalone selling price remains shrouded in mystery for the enigmatic Product A, impervious to estimation. Behold Product B, with an awe-inspiring standalone selling price of $250. Now, set your gaze upon the total of their combined offering, a price of $360. Amidst this transaction, the inquiry arises: What portion of this price shall be allocated to the performance obligation for delivering Product B? A. $110 B. $250 C. $360 D. $610

Dynamo Electronics, a renowned electronics manufacturer, has introduced an exclusive promotion for its cutting-edge tablet device - an extended service plan at a reduced rate. The standard price of the service plan is $400, but Dynamo is offering it for $320 when purchased with the tablet. They estimate a 60% likelihood that customers will opt for the discounted extended service plan when buying the tablet. Assuming Dynamo sells 500 tablets with the extended service plan discount offer, what would be the total stand-alone selling price allocated to the extended service plan option in these 500 tablet contracts? A. $24,000 B. $48,000 C. $96,000 D. $120,000

Amazonia Solutions operates a platform that connects stores around the world. When the customer tries to find a product online on Amazonia Solutions, and there isn’t that product, Amazonia connects the customer with other associated stores and receives a 15% fee commission from the other stores for facilitating the connection. One customer went through Amazonia and agreed to pay $8,000 for a product in Unga Bunga Store. How would this transaction be reflected on Amazonia Solutions’ income statement? A. Revenue of $8,000 B. Revenue of $8,000 and cost of services of $6,800 C. Revenue of $1,200 D. Revenue of $9,200 and cost of services of $8,000
quizSrc.definition: C. Revenue of $1,200

quizSrc.term: Meet Harmony, an upscale boutique offering elegant gift cards redeemable for luxurious products. In 2025, Harmony sold $4,500,000 worth of gift cards, and customers redeemed $4,000,000 of the gift cards for their desired items. By December 31, 2025, Harmony determined that $250,000 of the remaining gift cards would unlikely be redeemed. How much gift card revenue should Harmony recognize in 2025? A. $4,000,000 B. $4,500,000 C. $4,750,000 D. $4,250,000

ABC Company enters into a consignment arrangement with XYZ Corporation to sell its products. The products are delivered to XYZ's premises, but ownership remains with ABC until the products are sold to end customers. Which of the following statements regarding revenue recognition in this consignment arrangement is correct? A. ABC Company recognizes revenue when the products are delivered to XYZ Corporation's premises. B. ABC Company recognizes revenue when the products are sold to end customers. C. ABC Company recognizes revenue when the consignment arrangement is established. D. ABC Company does not recognize revenue until ownership of the products transfers to XYZ Corporation.
quizSrc.definition: B. ABC Company recognizes revenue when the products are sold to end customers.

quizSrc.term: XYZ Corporation sold a product to a customer for $10,000. The customer has the option to pay the full amount upfront or make equal annual payments over a period of three years. XYZ Corporation uses a discount rate of 8% to assess the time value of money. Which of the following statements regarding revenue recognition and time value of money is correct? A. XYZ Corporation recognizes the full $10,000 as revenue upfront, regardless of the payment option chosen by the customer. B. XYZ Corporation recognizes revenue based on the total discounted cash flows, considering the time value of money. C. XYZ Corporation recognizes revenue evenly over the three-year period, regardless of the payment option chosen by the customer. D. XYZ Corporation does not recognize revenue until the customer makes the final payment.
quizSrc.definition: B. XYZ Corporation recognizes revenue based on the total discounted cash flows, considering the time value of money.

quizSrc.term: Company XYZ enters into a long-term software development contract with a customer. The contract is expected to span over two years and has a total contract value of $500,000. According to US GAAP guidelines, if the outcome of the contract cannot be reasonably estimated, which of the following methods is required for revenue recognition in long-term contracts? A. Completed Contract Method B. Percentage-of-Completion Method C. Installment Method D. Cost Recovery Method
quizSrc.definition: A. Completed Contract Method

quizSrc.term: Under US GAAP, how is Construction in Progress (CIP) typically classified and reported for long-term contracts? A. CIP is classified as an intangible asset on the balance sheet. B. CIP is classified as a current liability on the balance sheet. C. CIP is classified as a long-term liability on the balance sheet. D. CIP is classified as a long-term asset on the balance sheet.
quizSrc.definition: D. CIP is classified as a long-term asset on the balance sheet.

quizSrc.term: Company XYZ is engaged in a long-term construction project and has a contract with a customer for $2,000,000. As of the end of the reporting period, the company has billed the customer for $1,200,000 based on the progress of the project. According to US GAAP, how should the progress billings be reported in the financial statements? A. Recognize the $1,200,000 as a contra asset CIP account based on the percentage of completion method. B. Recognize a portion of the $1,200,000 as revenue based on the percentage of completion method. C. Recognize the full $1,200,000 as revenue in the current reporting period. D. Recognize the full $1,200,000 as a liability in the current reporting period.
quizSrc.definition: A. Recognize the $1,200,000 as a contra asset CIP account based on the percentage of completion method.

quizSrc.term: In the construction industry, Kyle Engineering Inc. has secured a contract to build a state-of-the-art bridge at a contracted price of $18,000,000. The company follows the percentage-of-completion method to recognize revenue over time. Here are the project updates: Cost Incurred 2023 $4,500,000 2024 $1,500,000 2025 $3,750,000 Estimated Additional Cost to Complete 2023 $9,000,000 2024 $6,750,000 2025 $0 What is the company’s percentage completion in 2023? A. 10% B. 20% C. 33% D. 50%

The company follows the percentage-of-completion method to recognize revenue over time. Here are the project updates: Cost Incurred 2023 $4,500,000 2024 $1,500,000 2025 $3,750,000 Estimated Additional Cost to Complete 2023 $9,000,000 2024 $6,750,000 2025 $0 What is the company’s revenue recognized in 2024? A. $3,000,000 B. $6,000,000 C. $2,460,000 D. $18,000,000

In the construction industry, Kyle Engineering Inc. has secured a contract to build a state-of-the-art bridge at a contracted price of $18,000,000. The company follows the percentage-of-completion method to recognize revenue over time. Here are the project updates: Cost Incurred 2023 $4,500,000 2024 $7,500,000 2025 $7,500,000 Estimated Additional Cost to Complete 2023 $9,000,000 2024 $6,750,000 2025 $0 What is the company’s profit/(loss) recognized in 2024? A. $5,520,000 B. $6,000,000 C. ($2,250,000) D. ($750,000)

Under US GAAP, how are losses for long-term contracts recognized? A. Losses are not recognized for long-term contracts in US GAAP. B. Losses are recognized proportionally over the life of the contract based on the percentage of completion. C. Losses are recognized immediately when identified, regardless of the stage of completion. D. Losses are recognized only upon completion of the contract.
quizSrc.definition: C. Losses are recognized immediately when identified, regardless of the stage of completion.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following statements describes a difference between revenue recognition in US GAAP and IFRS? A. US GAAP allows for more flexibility in revenue recognition timing compared to IFRS. B. US GAAP requires revenue to be recognized at the point of delivery, while IFRS allows for revenue recognition over time. C. US GAAP and IFRS have identical revenue recognition principles and requirements. D. US GAAP recognizes revenue when it is realized or realizable, while IFRS recognizes revenue when it is probable and can be reliably measured.
quizSrc.definition: D. US GAAP recognizes revenue when it is realized or realizable, while IFRS recognizes revenue when it is probable and can be reliably measured.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following statements accurately describes the difference between revenue recognition at a certain point in time and revenue recognition over a period of time? A. Revenue recognition at a certain point in time and revenue recognition over a period of time are the same concepts and do not have any differences. B. Revenue recognition at a certain point in time recognizes revenue based on the transfer of control, while revenue recognition over a period of time recognizes revenue when cash is received. C. Revenue recognition at a certain point in time recognizes revenue when goods or services are delivered, while revenue recognition over a period of time recognizes revenue proportionally over the duration of a long-term contract. D. Revenue recognition at a certain point in time recognizes revenue when cash is received, while revenue recognition over a period of time recognizes revenue when the right to receive payment is established.
quizSrc.definition: C. Revenue recognition at a certain point in time recognizes revenue when goods or services are delivered, while revenue recognition over a period of time recognizes revenue proportionally over the duration of a long-term contract.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is not true about revenue recognition with respect to long-term construction contracts under US GAAP? A. Long-term construction contracts are often viewed as having multiple performance obligations due to the inclusion of different types of goods or services for each project. B. Long-term construction contracts often satisfy the criteria for recognizing revenue over time. C. Long-term construction contracts require accounting for construction in progress as well as billings to customers. D. Long-term construction contracts have multiple performance obligations as the goods or services are separately identifiable.
quizSrc.definition: D. Long-term construction contracts have multiple performance obligations as the goods or services are separately identifiable.

Which of the following statements accurately describes the difference between cash and accounts receivable? A. Cash refers to the amount of money owed to suppliers, while accounts receivable refers to funds available for immediate use. B. Cash refers to the amount of money owed to suppliers, while accounts receivable refers to funds available for immediate use. C. Cash is a type of asset that includes physical money and funds available in bank accounts, while accounts receivable refers to money owed by customers for goods or services. D. Cash is a type of asset that includes physical money and funds available in bank accounts, while accounts receivable refers to money owed by customers for goods or services. E. Cash represents the amount of money owed by customers, while accounts receivable represents actual physical currency. F. Cash represents the amount of money owed by customers, while accounts receivable represents actual physical currency. G. Cash and accounts receivable are interchangeable terms used to describe money owed by customers. H. Cash and accounts receivable are interchangeable terms used to describe money owed by customers.
quizSrc.definition: C. Cash is a type of asset that includes physical money and funds available in bank accounts, while accounts receivable refers to money owed by customers for goods or services. D. Cash is a type of asset that includes physical money and funds available in bank accounts, while accounts receivable refers to money owed by customers for goods or services.

Credit to sales returns for $20,000 D. Credit to sales returns for $20,000 E. Credit to refund liability for $20,000 F. Credit to refund liability for $20,000 G. Debit to refund liability for $20,000 H. Debit to refund liability for $20,000
quizSrc.definition: E. Credit to refund liability for $20,000 F. Credit to refund liability for $20,000

quizSrc.term: Which of the following statements accurately describes the differences between the direct write-off method and the allowance method for recording bad debt expense? A. The direct write-off method matches expenses with revenues, while the allowance method violates the matching principle. B. The direct write-off method matches expenses with revenues, while the allowance method violates the matching principle. C. The direct write-off method is required for tax purposes, while the allowance method is used for financial reporting. D. The direct write-off method is required for tax purposes, while the allowance method is used for financial reporting. E. The direct write-off method is based on estimates, while the allowance method relies on specific identification of uncollectible accounts. F. The direct write-off method is based on estimates, while the allowance method relies on specific identification of uncollectible accounts. G. The direct write-off method reduces accounts receivable directly, while the allowance method uses a contra-asset account. H. The direct write-off method reduces accounts receivable directly, while the allowance method uses a contra-asset account.
quizSrc.definition: G. The direct write-off method reduces accounts receivable directly, while the allowance method uses a contra-asset account. H. The direct write-off method reduces accounts receivable directly, while the allowance method uses a contra-asset account.

Weighted Average E. FIFO F. FIFO G. None of these answer choices are correct. H. None of these answer choices are correct.

quizSrc.term: When inventory costs are increasing: A. FIFO produces lower COGS, higher current Net Income, and higher Ending Inventory. B. FIFO produces lower COGS, higher current Net Income, and higher Ending Inventory. C. FIFO produces lower COGS, higher current Net Income, and lower Ending Inventory. D. FIFO produces lower COGS, higher current Net Income, and lower Ending Inventory. E. FIFO produces higher COGS, lower current Net Income, and higher Ending Inventory. F. FIFO produces higher COGS, lower current Net Income, and higher Ending Inventory. G. FIFO produces higher COGS, lower current Net Income, and lower Ending Inventory. H. FIFO produces higher COGS, lower current Net Income, and lower Ending Inventory.
quizSrc.definition: A. FIFO produces lower COGS, higher current Net Income, and higher Ending Inventory. B. FIFO produces lower COGS, higher current Net Income, and higher Ending Inventory.

Which of the following is not an advantage of the dollar-value LIFO method? A. Acquiring new items is viewed as replacing the dollar value of old items B. quizSrc.term:
Archie Co. purchased a framing machine for $53,000 on January 1, 2024. The machine is expected to have a four-year life, with a residual value of $5,000 at the end of four years. 

Using the straight-line method, depreciation for 2024 and book value at December 31, 2024, would be: A. $13,000 and $36,000 respectively. B. $13,000 and $36,000 respectively. C. $13,000 and $41,000 respectively. D. $13,000 and $41,000 respectively. E. $12,000 and $36,000 respectively. F. $12,000 and $36,000 respectively. G. $12,000 and $41,000 respectively. H. $12,000 and $41,000 respectively.
quizSrc.definition: G. $12,000 and $41,000 respectively. H. $12,000 and $41,000 respectively.

quizSrc.term:
A company purchased an asset on May 1, 2022, for $150,000 with an estimated three-year life and $10,000 residual value. The company uses units-of-production depreciation and expects the asset to produce 25,000 units. Calculate the gain or loss if the asset was sold on August 31, 2024, for $105,000. Actual production was: 2022 = 2,500 units; 2023 = 10,000 units; 2024 = 6,500 units. 

Which of the following options shows the correct gain or loss on the sale of the asset using the given information? A. Gain of $61,400 B. Gain of $61,400 C. Loss of $25,000 D. Loss of $25,000 E. Loss of $61,400 F. Loss of $61,400 G. Gain of $25,000 H. Gain of $25,000
quizSrc.definition: A. Gain of $61,400 B. Gain of $61,400

quizSrc.term: Lucas Corp. owns equipment for which it paid $60 million. At the end of 2022, it had accumulated depreciation on the equipment of $25 million. Due to adverse economic conditions, Lucas’ management determined that it should assess whether an impairment loss should be recognized for the equipment. The estimated undiscounted future cash flows to be provided by the equipment total $45 million, and the equipment's fair value at that point is $35 million. Under these circumstances, Lucas: A. Should recognize an impairment loss of $15 million B. Should recognize an impairment loss of $15 million C. Should recognize an impairment loss of $5 million D. Should recognize an impairment loss of $5 million E. Should recognize an impairment loss of $10 million F. Should recognize an impairment loss of $10 million G. Should not recognize an impairment loss H. Should not recognize an impairment loss
quizSrc.definition: G. Should not recognize an impairment loss H. Should not recognize an impairment loss

The company utilizes the straight-line depreciation method. However, in 2026, the estimate of the equipment's residual value was revised to zero. quizSrc.term: Under U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (US GAAP), how are debt investments initially recognized and subsequently measured? A. Debt investments are initially recognized at fair value and subsequently measured at fair value through other comprehensive income. B. Debt investments are initially recognized at fair value and subsequently measured at fair value through other comprehensive income. C. Debt investments are initially recognized at historical cost and subsequently measured at fair value or amortized cost. D. Debt investments are initially recognized at historical cost and subsequently measured at fair value or amortized cost. E. Under U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (US GAAP), how are debt investments initially recognized and subsequently measured? F. Under U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (US GAAP), how are debt investments initially recognized and subsequently measured? G. Debt investments are initially recognized at amortized cost and subsequently measured at fair value. H. Debt investments are initially recognized at amortized cost and subsequently measured at fair value.
quizSrc.definition: C. Debt investments are initially recognized at historical cost and subsequently measured at fair value or amortized cost. D. Debt investments are initially recognized at historical cost and subsequently measured at fair value or amortized cost.

quizSrc.term: Which statements accurately describe a difference between debt and equity investments? A. Debt investments are reported at fair value, while equity investments are reported at historical cost. B. Debt investments are reported at fair value, while equity investments are reported at historical cost. C. Debt investments provide a fixed rate of return, while equity investments provide potential for both income and capital appreciation. D. Debt investments provide a fixed rate of return, while equity investments provide potential for both income and capital appreciation. E. Debt investments are classified as current assets, while equity investments are classified as long-term liabilities. F. Debt investments are classified as current assets, while equity investments are classified as long-term liabilities. G. Debt investments represent ownership interests in a company, while equity investments represent loans made to a company. H. Debt investments represent ownership interests in a company, while equity investments represent loans made to a company.
quizSrc.definition: C. Debt investments provide a fixed rate of return, while equity investments provide potential for both income and capital appreciation. D. Debt investments provide a fixed rate of return, while equity investments provide potential for both income and capital appreciation.

quizSrc.term: In financial reporting, what is the key difference between the stated rate and the market rate? A. The stated rate is used to calculate the interest paid on a financial instrument, while the market rate reflects the prevailing interest rates in the market. B. The stated rate is used to calculate the interest paid on a financial instrument, while the market rate reflects the prevailing interest rates in the market. C. The stated rate always increases over time, while the market rate fluctuates based on economic conditions. D. The stated rate always increases over time, while the market rate fluctuates based on economic conditions. E. The stated rate applies to debt instruments, while the market rate applies to equity instruments. F. The stated rate applies to debt instruments, while the market rate applies to equity instruments. G. The government sets the stated rate, while the market rate is determined by supply and demand. H. The government sets the stated rate, while the market rate is determined by supply and demand.
quizSrc.definition: A. The stated rate is used to calculate the interest paid on a financial instrument, while the market rate reflects the prevailing interest rates in the market. B. The stated rate is used to calculate the interest paid on a financial instrument, while the market rate reflects the prevailing interest rates in the market.

quizSrc.term: Cema Inc. acquires bonds with a maturity value of $900,000 for $870,000. How would Cema record the purchase of these bonds in its journal entry? A. quizSrc.definition: G. Credit of $30,000 to Discount on bond investment H. Credit of $30,000 to Discount on bond investment

quizSrc.term: What are the key differences between trading, available-for-sale, and held-to-maturity debt investments in US GAAP? A. The classification is based on the investor's intent, the recognition of unrealized gains or losses, and the expected holding period. C. The classification is based on the investment's original cost, the market value at the reporting date, and the expected return on the investment. D. The classification is based on the investment's original cost, the market value at the reporting date, and the expected return on the investment. E. The classification is based on the investor's industry sector, the level of diversification in the investment portfolio, and the maturity of the investment. F. The classification is based on the investor's industry sector, the level of diversification in the investment portfolio, and the maturity of the investment. G. The classification is based on the type of interest rate (fixed or variable), the investment's credit rating, and the investment's marketability. H. The classification is based on the type of interest rate (fixed or variable), the investment's credit rating, and the investment's marketability.
quizSrc.definition: A. The classification is based on the investor's intent, the recognition of unrealized gains or losses, and the expected holding period. B. The classification is based on the investor's intent, the recognition of unrealized gains or losses, and the expected holding period.

The initial values of the building, land, and equipment would be: A. $4,000,000; $2,500,000; $1,500,000 B. $4,000,000; $2,500,000; $1,500,000 C. $3,600,000; $2,400,000; $2,000,000 D. $3,600,000; $2,400,000; $2,000,000 E. $4,500,000; $3,000,000; $500,000 F. $4,500,000; $3,000,000; $500,000 G. $4,500,000; $3,000,000; $2,500,000 H. $4,500,000; $3,000,000; $2,500,000

Fair value, only if higher than book value of the acquired company. D. Fair value, only if higher than book value of the acquired company. E. Fair value of the net assets acquired. F. Fair value of the net assets acquired. G. Book value of the acquired company. H. Book value of the acquired company.
quizSrc.definition: E. Fair value of the net assets acquired. F. Fair value of the net assets acquired.

quizSrc.term: Under U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (US GAAP), what is an asset retirement obligation (ARO)? A. A reserve established to account for potential losses on financial investments. B. A reserve established to account for potential losses on financial investments. C. A capital expenditure incurred for the acquisition of new assets. D. A capital expenditure incurred for the acquisition of new assets. E. A liability that represents the estimated costs for the retirement of tangible fixed assets. F. A liability that represents the estimated costs for the retirement of tangible fixed assets. G. An intangible asset that represents the future benefits from the retirement of long-term assets. H. An intangible asset that represents the future benefits from the retirement of long-term assets.
quizSrc.definition: E. A liability that represents the estimated costs for the retirement of tangible fixed assets. F. A liability that represents the estimated costs for the retirement of tangible fixed assets.

quizSrc.term: ABC Company has been using the Average Cost inventory method for several years but is considering changing to the LIFO (Last-In, First-Out) method. Which of the following statements is a potential advantage of switching to the LIFO method? A. LIFO enhances financial statement comparability across different companies. B. LIFO enhances financial statement comparability across different companies. C. LIFO simplifies recordkeeping and cost calculations. D. LIFO simplifies recordkeeping and cost calculations. E. LIFO results in lower taxable income during periods of rising prices. F. LIFO results in lower taxable income during periods of rising prices. G. LIFO provides a better reflection of the physical flow of inventory. H. LIFO provides a better reflection of the physical flow of inventory.
quizSrc.definition: E. LIFO results in lower taxable income during periods of rising prices. F. LIFO results in lower taxable income during periods of rising prices.

quizSrc.term: XYZ Corporation has been using the First-In, First-Out (FIFO) method to value its inventory for several years but is considering changing to the Weighted Average Cost method. Which of the following statements is a potential advantage of switching to the Weighted Average Cost method? A. Weighted Average Cost enhances financial statement comparability across different companies. B. Weighted Average Cost enhances financial statement comparability across different companies. C. Weighted Average Cost simplifies recordkeeping and cost calculations. D. Weighted Average Cost simplifies recordkeeping and cost calculations. E. Weighted Average Cost results in lower taxable income during periods of falling prices. F. Weighted Average Cost results in lower taxable income during periods of falling prices. G. Weighted Average Cost provides a better reflection of the physical flow of inventory. H. Weighted Average Cost provides a better reflection of the physical flow of inventory.
quizSrc.definition: A. Weighted Average Cost enhances financial statement comparability across different companies. B. Weighted Average Cost enhances financial statement comparability across different companies.

quizSrc.term: When comparing International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) and Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) regarding the valuation of inventories, which of the following statements is true? A. IFRS allows the use of either LIFO (Last-In, First-Out) or FIFO (First-In, First-Out) methods, while U.S. GAAP only permits the use of FIFO. B. IFRS allows the use of either LIFO (Last-In, First-Out) or FIFO (First-In, First-Out) methods, while U.S. GAAP only permits the use of FIFO. C. Both IFRS and U.S. GAAP explicitly state the valuation method to be used for inventories. D. Both IFRS and U.S. GAAP explicitly state the valuation method to be used for inventories. E. U.S. GAAP permits the use of LIFO, while IFRS prohibits the use of LIFO. F. U.S. GAAP permits the use of LIFO, while IFRS prohibits the use of LIFO. G. Both IFRS and US GAAP require the use of the same inventory valuation methods. H. Both IFRS and US GAAP require the use of the same inventory valuation methods.
quizSrc.definition: E. U.S. GAAP permits the use of LIFO, while IFRS prohibits the use of LIFO. F. U.S. GAAP permits the use of LIFO, while IFRS prohibits the use of LIFO.

quizSrc.term: Under US GAAP, which of the following statements regarding fair value through net income (FVTNI) classification for equity investments is true? A. The FVTNI classification is primarily used for long-term investments held by financial institutions. B. The FVTNI classification is primarily used for long-term investments held by financial institutions. C. The FVTNI classification allows for the recognition of dividends received from the equity investment. E. The FVTNI classification requires the equity investment to be measured at fair value with changes recorded in the income statement.
quizSrc.definition: G. The FVTNI classification requires the equity investment to be measured at fair value with changes recorded in the income statement. H. The FVTNI classification requires the equity investment to be measured at fair value with changes recorded in the income statement.

quizSrc.term: The equity method is used when an investor can't control, but can exercise significant influence over the operating and financial policies of the investee. We presume, in the absence of evidence to the contrary, that this is so if: A. The investor owns between 20% and 50% of the investee's voting shares. B. The investor owns between 20% and 50% of the investee's voting shares. C. The investor classifies the investment as held-to-maturity. D. The investor classifies the investment as held-to-maturity. E. The investor classifies the investment as available-for-sale. F. The investor classifies the investment as available-for-sale. G. quizSrc.term: An interest-bearing note payable on the balance sheet of CMA Co. will require the following payments: A. Stated face amount at issuance along with periodic interest payments determined by stated interest rate. B. Stated face amount at issuance along with periodic interest payments determined by stated interest rate. C. Stated face amount at maturity along with periodic interest payments determined by stated interest rate. D. Stated face amount at maturity along with periodic interest payments determined by stated interest rate. E. Stated face amount at maturity along with periodic interest payments determined by the effective interest rate. F. Stated face amount at maturity along with periodic interest payments determined by the effective interest rate. G. Stated face amount at the beginning along with periodic interest payments determined by LIBOR + state’s interest rate. H. Stated face amount at the beginning along with periodic interest payments determined by LIBOR + state’s interest rate.
quizSrc.definition: C. Stated face amount at maturity along with periodic interest payments determined by stated interest rate. D. Stated face amount at maturity along with periodic interest payments determined by stated interest rate.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following statements accurately describes an installment bond? A. An installment bond does not require periodic payments of principal and interest. B. An installment bond does not require periodic payments of principal and interest. C. An installment bond only requires periodic payments of interest, not principal. D. An installment bond only requires periodic payments of interest, not principal. E. An installment bond involves periodic payments of both principal and interest on the loan. F. An installment bond involves periodic payments of both principal and interest on the loan. G. An installment bond requires the same payment amount for each period, with no variation in principal and interest allocation. H. An installment bond requires the same payment amount for each period, with no variation in principal and interest allocation.
quizSrc.definition: E. An installment bond involves periodic payments of both principal and interest on the loan. F. An installment bond involves periodic payments of both principal and interest on the loan.

quizSrc.term: The interest expense for the reporting period on a bond is calculated using ____________ rate and the cash payments are calculated using ________ rate. A. negotiated; coupon B. negotiated; coupon C. market; coupon D. market; coupon E. market; market F. market; market G. coupon; market H. coupon; market
quizSrc.definition: C. market; coupon D. market; coupon

The bonds were sold to yield 6%. The issue price of the bonds is (please round your answer to the nearest thousand): A. $1,085,000 B. $1,085,000 C. $1,769,000 D. $1,769,000 E. $1,779,000 F. $1,779,000 G. $1,999,000 H. $1,999,000

How much interest expense shall be recorded for the six months ended December 31, 2022, if any? A. $80,000 B. $80,000 C. $40,000 D. $40,000 E. $20,000 F. $20,000 G. $0 H. $0

On July 1, 2022, Illini Company sold 3-year $1 million 8% bonds for $1 million. The interest is payable semiannually on January 1 and July 1. No interim statements were prepared. Please answer the following question (base your calculation on the number of months).

What account is credited in the relevant journal entry recorded on December 31, 2022, if any? A. Cash B. Cash C. Interest expense D. Interest expense E. Interest payable F. Interest payable G. None H. None
quizSrc.definition: E. Interest payable F. Interest payable

quizSrc.term: For a sales-type lease, A. The sales price includes the present value of the unguaranteed residual value. B. The sales price includes the present value of the unguaranteed residual value. C. The present value of the guaranteed residual value is deducted to determine the cost of goods sold. D. The present value of the guaranteed residual value is deducted to determine the cost of goods sold. E. The gross profit recognized at lease inception will be the same whether the residual value is guaranteed or unguaranteed. F. The gross profit recognized at lease inception will be the same whether the residual value is guaranteed or unguaranteed. G. None of these. H. None of these.
quizSrc.definition: E. The gross profit recognized at lease inception will be the same whether the residual value is guaranteed or unguaranteed. F. The gross profit recognized at lease inception will be the same whether the residual value is guaranteed or unguaranteed.

quizSrc.term: According to the lessor, a guaranteed residual value by the lessee should be: A. Included as part of the lease receivable only to the extent that guaranteed residual value is expected to exceed estimated residual value. B. Included as part of the lease receivable only to the extent that guaranteed residual value is expected to exceed estimated residual value. C. Excluded from lease payments. D. Excluded from lease payments. E. Included as part of the lease receivable at future value F. Included as part of the lease receivable at future value G. Included as part of the lease receivable at present value H. Included as part of the lease receivable at present value
quizSrc.definition: G. Included as part of the lease receivable at present value H. Included as part of the lease receivable at present value

quizSrc.term: From the perspective of the lessee, a guaranteed residual value indicates which of the following: A. A difference between the estimated residual value and guaranteed residual value has no impact on lessee accounting B. A difference between the estimated residual value and guaranteed residual value has no impact on lessee accounting C. The entire guaranteed residual value represents an additional cash payment for the lessee D. The entire guaranteed residual value represents an additional cash payment for the lessee E. If the guaranteed residual value exceeds the estimated residual value, the excess represents an additional estimated payment for the lessee. F. If the guaranteed residual value exceeds the estimated residual value, the excess represents an additional estimated payment for the lessee. G. All the above sentences are correct. H. All the above sentences are correct.
quizSrc.definition: E. If the guaranteed residual value exceeds the estimated residual value, the excess represents an additional estimated payment for the lessee. F. If the guaranteed residual value exceeds the estimated residual value, the excess represents an additional estimated payment for the lessee.

On January 1, 2022, Illini Company sold equipment with a carrying value of $30,000 to Prairie View Corp. and simultaneously leased it back for three years. The sale price was $38,656.45 (equal to the fair value). The remaining useful life was 3 years. The lease required three annual payments of $15,000 starting December 31, 2022. The implicit rate for the lease was 8%. Please answer the following the question. 

Should Illini record a sale on January 1, 2022? A. Yes B. Yes C. No D. No
quizSrc.definition: C. No D. No

Long-term debt that is callable by the lender in the upcoming year will not be classified as a current liability if the debt is not expected to be called. B. Current liabilities could include estimated amounts. D. Current liabilities could include estimated amounts. E. On January 1, 2022, Illini Company borrowed $20,000 from a local bank by signing a two-month 12% note. Interest was payable at maturity on February 28, 2022. Based on the information provided, please answer the following question (base your calculation on the number of months). 

What is the total interest expense Illini should record on the note? A. $1,200 B. $1,200 C. $2,400 D. $2,400 E. $0 F. $0 G. $400 H. $400

On January 1, 2022, Illini Company borrowed $20,000 from a local bank by signing a two-month 12% note. Interest was payable at maturity on February 28, 2022. Based on the information provided, please answer the following question (base your calculation on the number of months). 

What account(s) should be debited at maturity by Illini Company? A. Interest expense and Notes payable B. Interest expense and Notes payable C. Interest payable D. Interest payable E. Notes payable F. Notes payable G. Interest expense H. Interest expense
quizSrc.definition: A. Interest expense and Notes payable B. Interest expense and Notes payable

quizSrc.term:
Illini Inc. is billed quarterly for the cost of utilities. The bill for October – December 2022 is expected to be received and paid in January 2023. The average monthly cost for utilities is $1,500. 

Based on the information provided, how much utilities expense should be accrued as of December 31, 2022? A. $0 B. $0 C. $2,250 D. $2,250 E. $4,500 F. $4,500 G. $1,500 H. $1,500

Illini Inc. is billed quarterly for the cost of utilities. The bill for October – December 2022 is expected to be received and paid in January 2023. The average monthly cost for utilities is $1,500. 

When the bill is paid in January 2023, what account should be debited by Illini, Inc.? A. Interest expense B. Interest expense C. Cash D. Cash E. Notes payable F. Notes payable G. Utilities payable H. Utilities payable
quizSrc.definition: G. Utilities payable H. Utilities payable

On November 1, 2022, Big Grove Company collected $9.8 million cash from customers for products that would be delivered in January 2023. Based on the information provided, please answer the following question:  

As of the fiscal year ended December 31, 2022, what should Big Grove Company report related to the $9.8 million collected from customers? A. Liability of $9.8 million B. Liability of $9.8 million C. Receivable of $9.8 million D. Receivable of $9.8 million E. Revenues of $9.8 million F. Revenues of $9.8 million G. No balance should be recorded as of December 31, 2022. H. No balance should be recorded as of December 31, 2022.

When the product is provided to customers in January 2023, what account should be debited by Illini, Inc.? A. Cash B. Cash C. Deferred revenue D. Deferred revenue E. Revenue F. Revenue G. Accounts receivable H. Accounts receivable
quizSrc.definition: C. Deferred revenue D. Deferred revenue

quizSrc.term: At the fiscal year-end, suppose a loss is considered reasonably possible and estimated to be $30,000. A loss of $30,000 is considered material to the financial statements. The year-end financial statements should reflect which of the following: A. Disclosure note detailing the possible loss B. Disclosure note detailing the possible loss C. No accrual or disclosure is necessary. D. No accrual or disclosure is necessary. E. Accrued lost of $30,000 F. Accrued lost of $30,000 G. Accrued loss of $30,000 and a disclosure note detailing the possible loss H. Accrued loss of $30,000 and a disclosure note detailing the possible loss
quizSrc.definition: A. Disclosure note detailing the possible loss B. Disclosure note detailing the possible loss

How much did Illini spend on the warranty services in 2022? A. $4,000 B. $4,000 C. $2,000 D. $2,000 E. $8,000 F. $8,000 G. $28,000 H. $28,000

Illini’s incremental borrowing rate is 8%, which is the same as the lessor’s implicit rate.

Suppose the machine’s fair value on January 1, 2022, was $180,000. 

The classification of the lease is: A. Unable to determine based on the information provided. B. Unable to determine based on the information provided. C. An operating lease. D. An operating lease. E. A finance lease. F. A finance lease. G. A sales-type lease. H. A sales-type lease.

Illini’s incremental borrowing rate is 8%, which is the same as the lessor’s implicit rate.

Suppose the machine’s fair value on January 1, 2022, was $200,000.

Illini, Inc. reported income from continuing operations before tax of $114 million, in addition to a loss on discontinued operations of $40 million. The company’s tax rate is 25%.

A tax benefit of $10 million that is net against the $40 million pretax loss. D. A tax benefit of $10 million that is net against the $40 million pretax loss. E. quizSrc.definition: C. A tax benefit of $10 million that is net against the $40 million pretax loss. D. A tax benefit of $10 million that is net against the $40 million pretax loss.

quizSrc.term:
Illini, Inc. incurred a taxable loss for the current year (2022). The company had been profitable prior to 2022 and decided to use the carryback provisions to offset a portion of the prior period income. 

How should the amounts related to the carryback be reported in the 2022 financial statements? A. The tax refund should be shown as a reduction of the loss in 2022. B. The tax refund should be shown as a reduction of the loss in 2022. C. The tax refund should be reported as a deferred charge and amortized in future years. D. The tax refund should be reported as a deferred charge and amortized in future years. E. The tax refund should be reported as a prior period adjustment. F. The tax refund should be reported as a prior period adjustment. G. The tax refund should be reported as revenue in the current year. H. The tax refund should be reported as revenue in the current year.
quizSrc.definition: A. The tax refund should be shown as a reduction of the loss in 2022. B. The tax refund should be shown as a reduction of the loss in 2022.

On 1/1/20X1, Illini has 20,000 shares of $1 par common stock outstanding. On 1/1/20X1, Illini Company's executives have 1,000 vested stock options that were awarded as compensation before. These options permit them to buy 1,000 shares of the Illini's $1 par value common stock at an exercise price of $10. The fair value of these options on the original option grant date was estimated at $4 each. During 20X1 Illini Company reacquires 1,500 common shares as treasury shares as follows: 4/1/20X1 300 shares at $10 each 7/1/20X1 400 shares at $15 each 10/1/20X1 800 shares at $20 each On April 1, 20X1, Illini issues 1,000 shares of $100 par value 8% convertible cumulative preferred stock. The shares are sold at par value. These shares are convertible into 2,000 common shares. No dividends are declared in 20X1. On January 1, 20X2, the stock price is $18 per share, and 500 options are exercised. Assume that Illini reissues treasury shares to satisfy the executives' exercise of options, and that it is using the first-in first-out cost flow method. The average stock price in 20X1 and 20X2 are the same at $16 per share. Assume that there is a zero balance in the APIC– treasury stock account on 1/1/20X1. During 20X2, Illini Company also has the following transactions: Feb 1: Issues 1,000 shares of common stock for $15 per share. April 1: Issues 1,000 shares of common stock in exchange for the right to use a competitor’s brand when marketing its products. The stock trades at $16 per share on April 1, 20X2, and independent experts put the value of the brand between $10,000 and $20,000. Please use "brand asset" to record the right. September 1: Re-issues the remaining 1,000 shares of treasury stock at $16 per share, originally acquired in 20X1. October 1: Has a 2-for-1 stock split effected in a 100% stock dividend on all outstanding common shares on this date. Hint: record the transaction at the par value of the stock. Assume that the conversion ratios for outstanding convertible bonds and convertible preferred stock would double after the 2-for-1 stock split. December 31: Declares and pays cash dividends to both preferred and common stockholders. The dividends to common stock holders are 10 cents per share. Please refer to the instructions and the table in this question. Enter the correct journal entry for part [A].
